title: Flowise Faiss vector store writes to attacker-controlled basePath (path traversal)
description: In the Faiss vector store node (packages/components/nodes/vectorstores/Faiss/Faiss.ts), the upsert() method reads basePath = nodeData.inputs?.basePath as string from user-supplied vectorStoreConfig and passes it unsanitized to vectorStore.save(basePath). The same basePath is used in init() via FaissStore.load(basePath, embeddings). Because the value comes from the authenticated /api/v1/document-store/vectorstore/insert request and is never validated, an attacker with documentStores:upsert-config permission can supply an absolute or '../'-laden path and have the Faiss index (docstore.json, faiss.index) written to an arbitrary filesystem location. The sibling SimpleStore (LlamaIndex) node has the identical flaw.
